◆ RANGE GUIDE
CLOSE QUARTERS 3–25 ft Highest retained energy and the cleanest visual read. Best zone for dramatic, close-range console feedback.
PRIMARY ENVELOPE 25–50 ft Best balance of retained energy, consistency, and readable shot data. This is the clearest all-around operating envelope.
EXTENDED RANGE 50–75 ft Energy is tapering and spread matters more. The console should emphasize drop-off and lower confidence in this band.
MAXIMUM RANGE 75–100 ft Low-confidence range. Keep the presentation more cinematic than technical here, since consistency and retained energy fall off.

◆ DOCTRINE & FIELD CAVEATS · LLBC ZONE THRESHOLD TABLE v1.0 ▼
METHODOLOGY
Estimated thresholds based on published kinetic impact projectile research, community testing documentation, and anecdotal empirical reference points. Real-world results vary significantly based on target body composition, clothing layers, adrenaline state, shot placement precision, and engagement distance. These values represent average build targets in standard street clothing. Not clinical data.
ZONE THRESHOLD TABLE v1.0 — .68 CAL
ZoneMarginalModerateSevereIncapacitate
all values are impact joules at engagement distance
GLOBAL FIELD CAVEAT
Thresholds are estimates for average build targets (160-185 lb, standard street clothing). Heavily muscled subjects require significantly higher energy across all zones — thigh zone unreliable for incapacitation at any achievable .68 cal energy level. High body fat reduces chest and solar plexus effectiveness. High adrenaline state can override pain compliance at any threshold. These values are planning references, not clinical guarantees. Always assume the threat can continue until they demonstrably cannot.
PER-ZONE NOTES
Tap ⓘ INFO on any zone in the legend above to expand its full anatomical description, body-composition caveats, and threshold breakdown.
